summary: Rough but listenable soundboard captures an emotionally charged Dylan set highlighted by stellar renditions of Baby Blue, Memphis Blues Again and Ballad of the Thin Man.
review: The performance is praised for its heartfelt Dylan‑focused selections, with Evan Hunt calling the Baby Blue, Memphis Blues Again, and Ballad of the Thin Man versions all‑time keepers. The recording itself is described as a tad rough and somewhat inferior, but still adequate when turned up loud. Opinions on the tape are mixed, leading to an overall moderate rating.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong positive comments about the show offset by criticism of the soundboard's rough quality; three reviews with varying star scores produce a mid‑range average.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Described as rough, a bit muddy and inferior by one reviewer, but still adequate and enjoyable when turned up loud; lacks high fidelity but captures the live ambience.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A Dylan‑centric set that flows emotionally, with standout vocal and instrumental moments; reviewers highlight the seamless feel despite recording limitations.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for mid‑verse pleadings and a soaring lead break on Ballad of the Thin Man; also fills in admirably on Baby Blue outro.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboard) noted for filling in admirably on Baby Blue outro.
